items.title: Vance says no deal reached after marathon session of direct talks with Iran
items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance told reporters that "we have not reached an agreement" following face-to-face talks with Iranian leaders and Pakistani negotiators.
items.content: Vice President JD Vance told reporters that "we have not reached an agreement" following face-to-face talks with Iranian leaders and Pakistani negotiators.

items.title: US and Iran fail to reach a deal after peace talks in Islamabad
items.standfirst: The United States and Iran ended peace talks on Sunday without reaching a deal to end the war, leaving a fragile two-week ceasefire in doubt as both sides blamed each other for the breakdown and signaled little progress on core disputes.
items.content: The United States and Iran ended peace talks on Sunday without reaching a deal to end the war, leaving a fragile two-week ceasefire in doubt as both sides blamed each other for the breakdown and signaled little progress on core disputes.

items.title: No Deal: U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad collapse
items.standfirst: The United States and Iran failed to reach an agreement after a day of highly anticipated face-to-face peace talks, Washington's lead negotiator Vice President J.D. Vance announced on Sunday.
items.content: The United States and Iran failed to reach an agreement after a day of highly anticipated face-to-face peace talks, Washington's lead negotiator Vice President J.D. Vance announced on Sunday.(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)

items.title: US and Iran fail to reach deal after marathon talks
items.standfirst: Negotiations foundered over Tehran’s unwillingness to concede ground on nuclear weapons, says JD Vance
items.content: Negotiations foundered over Tehran’s unwillingness to concede ground on nuclear weapons, says JD Vance

items.title: US and Iran end 21-hour ceasefire talks without agreement before Vance departs Pakistan
items.standfirst: The United States and Iran ended a historic round of face-to-face talks early Sunday without reaching an agreement and the fate of the fragile, two-week ceasefire still unclear. Vice President JD Vance, who led the US delegation during the 21 hours of talks in Pakistan's capital Islamabad, said negotiations finished without a deal after the Iranians refused to accept American terms to refrain from developing a nuclear weapon. France24 correspondent Fraser Jackson shares his analysis from Washington.
items.content: The United States and Iran ended a historic round of face-to-face talks early Sunday without reaching an agreement and the fate of the fragile, two-week ceasefire still unclear. Vice President JD Vance, who led the US delegation during the 21 hours of talks in Pakistan's capital Islamabad, said negotiations finished without a deal after the Iranians refused to accept American terms to refrain from developing a nuclear weapon. France24 correspondent Fraser Jackson shares his analysis from Washington.

items.title: Iran-US: Talks ended after 21 hours without reaching agreement

items.title: Middle East war live: US failed to gain Tehran's trust, Iranian negotiator says
items.standfirst: ⁠Iran's ⁠Parliamentary Speaker Mohammad ​Baqer Qalibaf ​said Sunday that Iran raised "forward-looking" ​initiatives ‌during talks ⁠in Islamabad ‌but that the US failed ⁠to "earn" the trust of the Iranian delegation. US Vice President JD Vance said earlier that 21 hours of peace talks had failed to produce a deal to end the war. Read our live blog for all the latest developments.

items.title: Islamabad negotiations end without a deal – as it happened
items.standfirst: US vice-president says Iran chose not to accept American terms for a deal, including to not build nuclear weaponsJD Vance and US delegation leave Pakistan after failing to reach deal with IranUS officials claim Iran unable to find mines it laid in strait of HormuzThe UK will host a strait of Hormuz meeting next week, bringing together multiple countries aiming to restore free movement of ships through the strait, which has been blockaded by Iran since the beginning of the war and inflicted heavy damage on the global economy.A British official told AP that the meeting will oppose the idea of tolls being charged for passage through the waterway, as proposed by Iran as part of ceasefire negotiations. items.title: Iran-US negotiations underway in Pakistan, US says Strait of Hormuz being 'cleared'
items.standfirst: Delegations from the United States and Iran held direct negotiations on Saturday for the first time since 1979 in Islamabad,Pakistan, to end a six-week conflict, while US President Donald Trump said that the US military had begun clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Delegations from the United States and Iran held direct negotiations on Saturday for the first time since 1979 in Islamabad,Pakistan, to end a six-week conflict, while US President Donald Trump said that the US military had begun clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz.

clusters.title: Polls open in Hungary crunch vote
clusters.neutral_headline: Hungarians vote as PM Orban faces toughest election challenge in years
bullet_summary: Among Magyar's supporters, "one word keeps coming up: hope", said Casali
Reported by France24, Guardian World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Polls open in Hungary crunch vote
items.standfirst: Hungarians began voting on Sunday in closely watched parliamentary elections that could end nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year stint in power as a self-described "thorn" in the EU's side. France 24 correspondent Clovis Casali reports the latest from Budapest.
items.content: Hungarians began voting on Sunday in closely watched parliamentary elections that could end nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year stint in power as a self-described "thorn" in the EU's side. France 24 correspondent Clovis Casali reports the latest from Budapest.

items.title: Hungary: Orban and Magyar on the home stretch
items.standfirst: Hungarians headed to the polls on Sunday in a pivotal parliamentary election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year grip on power, as the opposition led by Peter Magyar challenges his nationalist rule in a contest drawing scrutiny across Europe and backing for Orban from US President Donald Trump. Lecturer in comparative politics at Keele University, Gemma Loomes, shares more insights on the vote.
items.content: Hungarians headed to the polls on Sunday in a pivotal parliamentary election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year grip on power, as the opposition led by Peter Magyar challenges his nationalist rule in a contest drawing scrutiny across Europe and backing for Orban from US President Donald Trump. Lecturer in comparative politics at Keele University, Gemma Loomes, shares more insights on the vote.

items.title: Among Magyar's supporters, "one word keeps coming up: hope"
items.standfirst: "This is a clear battle between two men, the incumbent, Prime Minister Viktor Orban and far right leader, and the opposition leader Peter Magyar, a conservative, pro-European figure," said FRANCE 24 Journalist Clovis Casali, reporting from Budapest, Hungary. Among Magyar's supporters, "one word keeps coming up: hope", said Casali. Click on the video for more.
items.content: "This is a clear battle between two men, the incumbent, Prime Minister Viktor Orban and far right leader, and the opposition leader Peter Magyar, a conservative, pro-European figure," said FRANCE 24 Journalist Clovis Casali, reporting from Budapest, Hungary. Among Magyar's supporters, "one word keeps coming up: hope", said Casali. Click on the video for more.

items.title: Hungarians vote in hard-fought election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years
items.standfirst: Rightwing leader trails in polls to Péter Magyar, despite support from JD Vance on recent visitHungarians are heading to the ballot boxes to vote in a hard-fought parliamentary election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power and potentially reshape the central European country’s relations with the EU, Moscow and Washington.In the campaign, Orbán – the EU’s longest-serving leader – has trailed in the polls as he faces an unprecedented challenge from Péter Magyar, a former elite member of Orbán’s Fidesz party. Continue reading...

items.title: Artemis II astronauts welcomed home to Houston after historic moonshot
items.standfirst: The Artemis II astronauts flew back to the Johnson Space Center in Houston Saturday to cheers and applause from family members and hundreds of NASA workers.
items.content: The Artemis II astronauts flew back to the Johnson Space Center in Houston Saturday to cheers and applause from family members and hundreds of NASA workers.

items.title: Artemis II crew returns to Houston after successful mission
items.standfirst: The Artemis II astronauts spoke following the successful completion of their mission. They splashed down on Friday night off the coast of San Diego. Retired Lieutenant Colonel David Mahan joins CBS News to discuss.
items.content: The Artemis II astronauts spoke following the successful completion of their mission. They splashed down on Friday night off the coast of San Diego. Retired Lieutenant Colonel David Mahan joins CBS News to discuss.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts speak publicly for first time since successful moon mission

items.content: The Artemis II crew spoke publicly for the first time after their successful mission at the Ellington Field Joint Reserve Base in Houston, Texas.

items.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston

items.content: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

items.content: Four astronauts. One historic mission. Venturing further from Earth than ever before.

items.title: Artemis II Safely Returns to Earth After Historic Moon Mission
items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis crew of four astronauts returned to Earth and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean, capping humanity’s first return to the moon in more than 50 years. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: NASA’s Artemis crew of four astronauts returned to Earth and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean, capping humanity’s first return to the moon in more than 50 years. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Artemis II Astronauts Safely Return to Earth After Historic Flight Around the Moon
items.standfirst: After traveling a greater distance from Earth than any humans before them, the astronauts of Artemis II have safely returned home.
items.content: After traveling a greater distance from Earth than any humans before them, the astronauts of Artemis II have safely returned home.

items.title: NASA’s Artemis Crew Set to Splashdown After Moon Voyage
items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis II crew prepares for a splashdown after a historic trip around the moon. Laurie Leshin, former head of NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, discusses the risk the astronauts face on their return to Earth and what comes next for the Artemis program. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: NASA’s Artemis II crew prepares for a splashdown after a historic trip around the moon. Laurie Leshin, former head of NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, discusses the risk the astronauts face on their return to Earth and what comes next for the Artemis program. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Pro-Palestinian rally in London: Over 500 arrested
items.standfirst: Police in London arrested more than 500 pro-Palestinian protesters on Saturday during a demonstration in support of the banned group Palestine Action. The government designated the group a terrorist organization in July, but the ban is currently being challenged in court. Emily Boyle reports.
items.content: Police in London arrested more than 500 pro-Palestinian protesters on Saturday during a demonstration in support of the banned group Palestine Action. The government designated the group a terrorist organization in July, but the ban is currently being challenged in court. Emily Boyle reports.

items.title: More than 500 people arrested at Palestine Action rally in London

items.title: Union Berlin appoint first female head coach
items.standfirst: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.
items.content: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.

clusters.title: Peru votes for new president Sunday
clusters.neutral_headline: Peruvians to go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: Peru votes for new president Sunday
items.standfirst: Peruvians head to the polls on Sunday in a crowded and unpredictable presidential election, with crime-weary voters set to choose their ninth leader in a decade, in a context of deep frustration over corruption and rising violence. Producer, writer and journalist for Radio Programas del Perú, Martin Riepel, shares further insights on this vote.
items.content: Peruvians head to the polls on Sunday in a crowded and unpredictable presidential election, with crime-weary voters set to choose their ninth leader in a decade, in a context of deep frustration over corruption and rising violence. Producer, writer and journalist for Radio Programas del Perú, Martin Riepel, shares further insights on this vote.

items.title: Peruvians to go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability
items.standfirst: Crime and corruption top voter concerns in highly unpredictable election with 35 candidates for presidentPeruvians go to the polls on Sunday hoping to break a cycle of instability that has produced nine presidents in a decade as well as surging violent crime, corruption scandals and overwhelming distrust in institutions and politicians.About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congress – all from a ballot sheet measuring nearly half a metre, the longest in the country’s history. Continue reading...
items.content: Crime and corruption top voter concerns in highly unpredictable election with 35 candidates for presidentPeruvians go to the polls on Sunday hoping to break a cycle of instability that has produced nine presidents in a decade as well as surging violent crime, corruption scandals and overwhelming distrust in institutions and politicians.About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congress – all from a ballot sheet measuring nearly half a metre, the longest in the country’s history. Continue reading...

items.title: Man arrested over alleged damage to US military plane at Shannon Airport

items.content: A man has been arrested over alleged criminal damage of a US military plane at an Irish airport.

items.title: Man arrested for allegedly damaging US military aircraft in Shannon airport
items.standfirst: Suspect in 40s arrested after man apparently climbed on to USAF C130 Hercules transport plane on remote taxiway in County ClareA man has been arrested after entering an unauthorised area of an airport in the Republic of Ireland and allegedly causing damage to a US military aircraft, police have said.The suspect, aged in his 40s, was arrested for alleged criminal damage and remains in custody over the incident on Saturday at Shannon airport in County Clare. Continue reading...
items.content: Suspect in 40s arrested after man apparently climbed on to USAF C130 Hercules transport plane on remote taxiway in County ClareA man has been arrested after entering an unauthorised area of an airport in the Republic of Ireland and allegedly causing damage to a US military aircraft, police have said.The suspect, aged in his 40s, was arrested for alleged criminal damage and remains in custody over the incident on Saturday at Shannon airport in County Clare. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Russian drone strikes kill 2 in Ukraine ahead of holiday ceasefire
clusters.neutral_headline: Russian drone strikes kill 2 in Ukraine ahead of holiday ceasefire
bullet_summary: Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight into Saturday, local authorities reported
KYIV, Ukraine — Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight into Saturday, local authorities reported, ahead of a proposed ceasefire for Orthodox Easter
